what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG outline house icon using three stroked paths for the roof, body, and door.
observations: The SVG uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with width and height set to 24.
The icon has no fill and uses stroke="currentColor".
Stroke styling uses 1.5 width with round line caps and joins.
The drawing is made from three path elements: roof, house body, and centered door.
standouts: The icon geometry is reduced to roof, wall outline, and door strokes.
conventions: Follows common outline icon conventions: 24px canvas, no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded caps and joins.
Uses compact inline SVG markup without whitespace formatting.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present for standalone accessibility.
notes: A minimal reusable home/house outline icon asset.
questions: Is this SVG intended to be decorative only, or should consumers provide an accessible label?
purpose_vs_actual: The file path and name indicate a house icon, and the SVG content draws a house outline.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a small scalable house icon that can inherit text or UI color in a design system.
contribution: Contributes a compact, themeable house/home icon substrate with simple stroke geometry suitable for reuse in navigation, controls, or symbolic UI contexts.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 SVG house outline icon using currentColor stroke, no fill, and three path elements for roof, body, and door.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a house or home concept as a reusable design-system icon asset.
